PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-18-2022 Continued To Jul-13-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Vale, via Zoom, for Emmanuela Magarrell, Petitioner; Azita Rahim, via Zoom, for and with Christina Bryant, in court, Objector.

The court notes that Ms. Bryant had not been included in the petition but was later provided notice. Mr. Vale reports that his client believed that the decedent had relinquished all parental rights. The court then confirms with Ms. Rahim that her firm only represents Ms. Bryant and not American Research Bureau as shown in the caption of the objection filed on behalf of her client. The court advises Mr. Vale that if his client is appointed Administrator, it is inclined to order a full bond and queries counsel if he believes whether his client is bondable. Mr. Vale advises that he is unsure and requests a continuance to resolve that issue and to meet and confer with opposing counsel. Ms. Rahim advises that she is open to informal discussions.

The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to Jul-13-2022 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.
